instructions: Answer the following questions to test your knowledge before starting the experiment.

questions.question: What does ER diagram stand for?
In an ER diagram, how are entities typically represented?
How are relationships represented in an ER diagram?
What is a primary key in an ER diagram?
options: An attribute that can be NULL
An attribute that uniquely identifies an entity

questions.question: What does cardinality specify in a relationship?
options: The number of instances of one entity associated with another
The primary key of an entity

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a type of cardinality?
What is a weak entity?
options: An entity that cannot be uniquely identified without a relationship to another entity
An entity with only one attribute
An entity that has no relationships

questions.question: What does total participation mean in an ER diagram?
options: Some entities participate in the relationship
Every entity must participate in the relationship
No entities participate in the relationship
Only one entity participates in the relationship

questions.question: How is total participation represented in an ER diagram?
What is a composite attribute?
options: An attribute that can have multiple values
An attribute that can be divided into smaller sub-attributes
An attribute that is a primary key
An attribute that is derived from other attributes

questions.question: When mapping a 1:N relationship to a relational schema, where is the foreign key placed?
options: In the entity on the "one" side
In the entity on the "many" side

questions.question: How is a many-to-many (M:N) relationship mapped to a relational schema?
options: Foreign key in one of the entities
A separate relation with foreign keys from both entities

questions.question: What is a derived attribute?
options: An attribute that is a primary key
An attribute that can have multiple values
An attribute whose value can be calculated from other attributes

questions.question: In an ER diagram, how are attributes typically represented?
What is the purpose of mapping an ER diagram to a relational schema?
options: To convert the conceptual design into a logical database design

questions.question: What does ER stand for?
In an ER diagram, what does a rectangle represent?
What shape represents an attribute?
What shape represents a relationship?
What is a primary key?
options: A unique identifier for an entity

questions.question: What does a double rectangle represent?
What does a dashed oval represent?
